_sources.dynamicPokemonExpansion: github.com/DizzyEggg/Dynamic-Pokemon-Expansion (Skeli789 collaborates on this) -- species.h/moves.h/charmap.tbl used for name tables, 2026-07-22.
_sources.cfru: github.com/Skeli789/Complete-Fire-Red-Upgrade -- Unbound's own engine base (same author). Confirmed gWarp1/gEventObjects/gStringVar1-4/Var8000 addresses and the EventObject/WarpData struct layouts (coordinates, map bank/number) against this source, 2026-07-22. Its region_map_sections.h has '#ifndef UNBOUND //Modify this section' around the MAPSEC name list -- confirms Unbound has its own custom map name table NOT present in this repo, so location names still need live discovery.

_confidence_levels.firered-anchor: Vanilla FireRed address from the pokefirered decomp / community RAM maps. NOT confirmed for Unbound — verify against a live read before trusting.
_confidence_levels.unbound-verified: Confirmed against Unbound specifically (community GameShark code, or a live diff-scanner result that unambiguously matches a known value).
_confidence_levels.candidate: Found via diff-scanner from ONE observed action. Plausible but not cross-verified — could be coincidental or part of an unrelated adjacent field. Needs a second, differently-shaped test before trusting.

pointers.name: gSaveBlock1Ptr
pointers.note: LE u32 pointing into WRAM; money, player position, flags, vars live inside this struct

party.name: gPlayerParty
party.note2: Confirmed on real hardware 2026-07-22: decoded Lv/HP/stats matched the actual in-game party exactly.
party.note3: note3-5 (superseded, see note6): chased a species/moves decode bug through a checksum-formula theory, a wrong substructure-order table, then a wrong table-application direction -- none of it was the real bug.
party.note6: RESOLVED 2026-07-22: this build does NOT use standard Gen3 encryption/shuffling for the live party struct at all. Manually decrypted a raw hex dump and species was garbage at every position under every theory; meanwhile species read as plain, unencrypted, unshuffled correct data at the classic fixed offset 0x20 -- confirmed correct for every party member regardless of personality value. Matches CFRU's own `struct Pokemon` (include/pokemon.h) -- the live in-RAM party type, distinct from the save-file BoxPokemon format -- which stores Growth/Attacks/Condition/Misc in plaintext at their classic fixed offsets, never encrypted or shuffled. Gen3Decrypt.kt now just reads fields directly, no XOR/no reordering.
party.note: 6 x 100-byte structs, entirely plaintext (no encryption/shuffling) in this build. Level/HP/stats at offsets 0x54-0x63; species/item/experience/moves/pp at 0x20-0x37 (see unencryptedFields).

enemyParty.name: gEnemyParty
enemyParty.note: Battle only. Confirmed 2026-07-22: decoded species/nicknames matched the real enemy team.

overworldObjects.name: gEventObjects (struct EventObject, CFRU naming)
overworldObjects.note: Slot 0 is the player. Address confirmed 2026-07-22 two ways: (1) matches gEventObjects in Skeli789/Complete-Fire-Red-Upgrade (Unbound's own engine base) include/new/ram_locs.h, and (2) matches the diff-scanner test result below.

currentCoordsX.note: obj+0x10, struct Coords16 currentCoords.x from CFRU's include/global.fieldmap.h. Confirmed live 2026-07-22: single X-axis step changed the low byte 0x1F->0x1E.

currentCoordsY.note: obj+0x12, currentCoords.y. Confirmed live 2026-07-22: a pure south step changed 36->37, and facingDirection/movementDirection/movementActionId all shifted consistently with a completed southward step in the same diff.

previousCoordsX.note: obj+0x14, previousCoords.x. Confirmed live 2026-07-22 alongside currentCoordsX -- both sync to the same resting-tile value once a step animation completes, which is why they appeared identical in the diff test.

previousCoordsY.note: obj+0x16, previousCoords.y. Confirmed live 2026-07-22 alongside currentCoordsY.

facingDirection.note: obj+0x18, low nibble = facingDirection, high nibble = movementDirection, per CFRU's struct EventObject.

scriptVars.name: gSpecialVars (0x8000-0x800F)
scriptVars.note: Engine-level scratch variables used by map scripts. Likely where Unbound's custom mission/difficulty/story flags live, given it heavily extends the script system. Values are transient/context-dependent, not fixed per-var meanings.

anchors.name: playerName
anchors.note: Read as all zeros on real Unbound hardware 2026-07-22 — likely shifted, needs re-discovery via diff scanner.

anchors.name: rivalName
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: decodes to "Ryan", matches the player's real rival name.

anchors.name: currentMapBank
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: changed consistently and distinctly moving between buildings and routes. Also matches gWarp1.mapGroup (struct WarpData) from CFRU's include/new/ram_locs.h / include/global.h.

anchors.name: currentWarpId
anchors.note: gWarp1.warpId, per CFRU's struct WarpData. Likely only meaningful right after a warp transition, not continuous like currentMapBank/Number.

anchors.name: currentWarpX
anchors.note: gWarp1.x. Warp-destination coordinate, not a continuously-updated live position -- use overworldObjects.currentCoordsX for that instead.

anchors.name: playerPartyCount
anchors.note: gPlayerPartyCount per CFRU. Number of occupied party slots -- read this before assuming all 6 party slots are valid Pokemon.

anchors.name: regionMapSectionId
anchors.note: gMapHeader.regionMapSectionId (CFRU #defines this as gCurrentMapName -- include/new/ram_locs.h). Confirmed live 2026-07-22: Route 11=0x6F(111), Fallshore City=0x5E(94), Route 10=0x64(100) -- three distinct areas gave three distinct ids. Confirms Unbound fully remapped these from vanilla FireRed (vanilla 0x64 is Route 10 Fly-up, 0x5E is Celadon City -- neither matches), so there's no shortcut via the public table. Name lookup now comes from assets/unbound_map_names.json, a small hand-built id->name table grown from live testing (see that file).

anchors.name: cheatItemSlot
anchors.note: Target of Unbound item GameShark codes (e.g. 0x028A=Psychic Memory, 0x0250=Flying Gem). Likely a give-item/bag-add slot.

anchors.name: repelStepCount
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: using a Max Repel changed 0x00 -> 0xFA. 0xFA = 250 decimal, exactly the step count a Max Repel grants — strong, non-coincidental match. Try a regular Repel (should write 100/0x64) or Super Repel (200/0xC8) to double-confirm, and watch it count down while walking.

anchors.name: battleStateFlag
anchors.note: Changed 0x00 -> 0x40 on entering a wild battle (before attacking), diff-scanner result from a single test. Hypothesis: a battle type/state flag byte. To confirm: check the value across a trainer battle, a double battle, and after the battle ends (should return to 0x00) - if it's consistently the same shape per battle type, it's real.

anchors.name: fieldCounterNearRepel
anchors.note: Changed 0x04 -> 0x00 on entering a wild battle, diff-scanner result from a single test. Sits exactly 4 bytes after repelStepCount, so it may be an adjacent field in the same small struct rather than something battle-related. To confirm: watch it while walking with no repel active (does it count steps or encounters?), and see if it resets on other triggers besides battle entry.

anchors.name: stringBuffer0
anchors.note: gStringVar1 per CFRU's include/new/ram_locs.h (matches address exactly). Confirmed live 2026-07-22 decoding real text ('Wed. 9:39 PM', the start-menu clock) -- general-purpose script string var, reused for lots of UI text including possibly the location banner. Read right after crossing into a new map to test that specifically.

anchors.name: stringBuffer1
anchors.note: gStringVar2 per CFRU. Same location-banner test as stringBuffer0.

anchors.name: stringBuffer2
anchors.note: gStringVar3 per CFRU. Same location-banner test as stringBuffer0.

anchors.name: msgBoxString
anchors.note: gStringVar4 per CFRU (address matches; size still a guess since CFRU's #define doesn't state a length). Same location-banner test as stringBuffer0.
